title: ns-3 Network Simulation Series — LTE and mmWave Modules
summary: A series of network simulation papers building on the ns-3 discrete-event network simulator. The first paper introduces the LTE-LENA module, providing complete 4G LTE cellular network simulation within ns-3, including eNodeB/UE devices, EPC (Evolved Packet Core), MAC schedulers, and multi-layer protocol stack (PHY, MAC, RLC, PDCP, RRC). The second paper extends the LTE module with a 5G millimeter wave (mmWave) simulation module, adding customizable TDD frame structures, mmWave channel models (3-state propagation, MIMO beamforming), and mmWave-specific PHY and MAC layers for high-frequency cellular communication research.
instructionPreview: This task involves implementing two network simulation modules for the ns-3 discrete-event network simulator. The workspace contains the ns-3 C++ framework with core simulation engine, network stack, mobility models, spectrum/propagation modules, and build system (CMake).
instruction:
This task involves implementing two network simulation modules for the ns-3 discrete-event
network simulator. The workspace contains the ns-3 C++ framework with core simulation engine,
network stack, mobility models, spectrum/propagation modules, and build system (CMake).

You must implement in order:
1. **LTE-LENA module** (src/lte/): A complete 4G LTE cellular network simulation module with
   EPC, PHY/MAC/RLC/PDCP/RRC protocol layers, multiple MAC schedulers, and trace output.
2. **mmWave module** (src/mmwave/): A 5G millimeter wave extension of the LTE module with
   mmWave-specific PHY (TDD frame, OFDM), channel models (3-state, beamforming), and
   MAC (flexible TTI schedulers).

After each module, create a run.sh that builds ns-3, runs a reference simulation, and
outputs experiment metrics as EXPERIMENT_NAME: VALUE lines.

Build: cd /workspace && cmake -S . -B build && cmake --build build -j$(nproc)
The ns-3 build system auto-discovers modules in src/ via CMakeLists.txt.

moduleDag.project: ns-3 Network Simulation Series — LTE and mmWave Modules
moduleDag.description: A series of network simulation papers building on the ns-3 discrete-event network simulator. The first paper introduces the LTE-LENA module, providing complete 4G LTE cellular network simulation within ns-3, including eNodeB/UE devices, EPC (Evolved Packet Core), MAC schedulers, and multi-layer protocol stack (PHY, MAC, RLC, PDCP, RRC). The second paper extends the LTE module with a 5G millimeter wave (mmWave) simulation module, adding customizable TDD frame structures, mmWave channel models (3-state propagation, MIMO beamforming), and mmWave-specific PHY and MAC layers for high-frequency cellular communication research.

nodes.label: LTE-LENA: An LTE Module for ns-3
nodes.description: Complete 4G LTE cellular simulation module for ns-3. Implements the full LTE protocol stack including PHY (OFDMA DL, SC-FDMA UL), MAC (multiple scheduler algorithms), RLC (TM/UM/AM modes), PDCP, and RRC layers. Provides EPC network elements (SGW/PGW, MME, eNodeB S1 application) and helper classes for simulation setup and trace output.

nodes.label: 5G mmWave Module for ns-3
nodes.description: 5G millimeter wave simulation module extending the LTE architecture. Adds mmWave-specific PHY layer with customizable TDD frame structure, 3-state channel model (LOS/NLOS/outage), MIMO beamforming with pre-generated channel matrices, mmWave MAC with flexible TTI schedulers, and AMC for mmWave links. Enables end-to-end simulation of 5G mmWave networks.

edges.label: mmWave extends LTE module for 5G simulation
